improvement.summary: Prevent repository metadata from being represented as the Cloudflare deployment run start time in provenance attestations.
improvement.problem: The workflow supplied github.event.repository.updated_at as RUN_STARTED_AT. That timestamp describes repository metadata, not the start of the deployment invocation, and could create false temporal provenance.
behavior: Accept a candidate start timestamp only when its source is exactly workflow-captured.
Reject malformed timestamps and captured starts later than finish.
When the start source is absent or untrusted, collapse startedOn to finishedOn rather than inventing run duration.
Emit startTimeStatus so downstream evidence distinguishes captured timing from unavailable timing.

verification.strongest_feasible_proof: GitHub confirmed both source and test commits and returned no workflow runs for the test commit.

limits: The new tests have not executed in GitHub Actions.
The current workflow does not yet capture and label an actual run-start timestamp, so provenance will conservatively report zero observed duration.
Temporal provenance does not prove deployment success, served identity, scientific truth, diagnosis, or treatment efficacy.
